extraction.one_line_summary: ROGLIČ Primož attacked the final mountain TT to overturn 26-second deficit, winning by 40 seconds and taking the Giro by 14 seconds over THOMAS.
tactical_patterns: ROGLIČ survived mid-climb mechanical without terminal time loss, revealing superior climbing reserves allowed recovery from disaster.
THOMAS's longer bike-change stop including helmet swap suggests equipment strategy miscalculation that cost crucial seconds.
KUSS setting early reference time signals Jumbo-Visma used domestique to provide real-time pacing intelligence for ROGLIČ.

rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Overcame mechanical mid-TT and still won by 40 seconds, showing exceptional climbing depth beyond just pacing discipline
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: Lost 40 seconds on 19km mountain TT despite only 26-second deficit, revealing age/fatigue caught up in sustained steep gradients
rider_signals.McNULTY Brandon: positive: Already stage winner earlier, posted competitive TT time suggesting UAE's climbing TT strength deeper than leader alone
rider_signals.POZZOVIVO Domenico: negative: Led early then suffered mechanical/fade, indicating older climbers particularly vulnerable on sustained 20%+ ramps
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: neutral: Lost time but secured Portugal's first GT podium since 1970s, suggesting consistency over explosiveness
